text.title: tool-migration-basics Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for tool-migration-basics. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Complete a pre-migration audit before any data movement: count total issues, attachments (by byte), custom fields, automation rules, and integrations; classify each integration as critical or non-critical.
rule.rationale: Skipping the audit produces mid-migration surprises — attachment size limits exceed target storage, custom fields have no target equivalent, critical integrations break with no rollback plan.

rule.statement: Every source field must be explicitly mapped to a target field OR explicitly marked as "won't migrate" with a documented reason; unmapped fields that silently drop data create post-cutover blame cycles.
rule.rationale: Documenting every "won't migrate" decision pre-empts post-cutover disputes about missing data. Silence is not agreement.

rule.statement: Preserve source IDs in a "legacy_id" custom field on the target tool; external systems that hardcoded source issue IDs (commit messages, bookmarks, documentation links) still resolve.
rule.rationale: ID changes (PROJ-1234 → ABC-5) break every external link simultaneously. The legacy_id field is the only safe bridge.

rule.statement: Map status fields explicitly with a lookup table showing source state, target state, and any behavioral differences; "Resolved" in Jira may mean approved-not-yet-deployed while the target tool's "Done" means shipped.
rule.rationale: Status semantics rarely map 1:1. Reviewing each mapping for behavioral differences prevents silent reinterpretation of work state.

rule.statement: Run the migration three times: dev-environment test, full dry-run with rollback on a clone of production, real cutover; each run catches a different class of bug.
rule.rationale: Dev test catches logic errors. Full dry-run catches rate limits, size limits, and data-edge cases that only appear at scale. Real cutover catches timing and concurrency issues under live load.

rule.statement: Freeze the source tool 4 hours before cutover; route new tickets created during the window to a known "intake" queue and re-key them by hand after cutover.
rule.rationale: Issues created during migration create reconciliation complexity. A short freeze with a manual intake queue is simpler and less risky than attempting live-sync.

rule.statement: Validate three numbers before declaring migration successful: total issue count, total comment count, and total attachment volume by byte.
rule.rationale: Three independent counts at different granularities catch distinct failure modes: issues lost, comments orphaned, and attachments silently dropped at size limits.

rule.statement: Manually sample-validate 50 random issues by hand, comparing source to target field-by-field; automated count validation does not catch field-content corruption.
rule.rationale: Status mapping bugs, Markdown-to-HTML conversion failures, and @mention resolution failures appear in field content, not in counts.

rule.statement: Keep the source tool read-only for at least 30 days after cutover; users will need to look up historical context for months after the switch.
rule.rationale: Post-cutover lookup requests are predictable and frequent. Read-only access at no cost is safer than full decommission and cheaper than rebuilding access when needed.

rule.statement: Human-in-the-loop checkpoints are mandatory at: mapping approval, cutover go/no-go decision, and rollback decisions.
rule.rationale: Each is an irreversible or high-impact decision. Agent errors at these gates compound into expensive remediation.
